display.purpose: Page titles, modal titles, section heads. Bricolage Grotesque with -0.025em tracking — never default tracking.

body.purpose: Default surface text. Cap line length at 65–75ch in long-form contexts.

label.purpose: Form labels paired with the field-group accent dot. Always Geist 500.

mono.purpose: Sensitive content only — passwords, recovery phrases, key fingerprints, command palette accelerators. The 0.06em tracking is the security cue.

shadows.name: glass-card-light
shadows.purpose: The signature glass card surface in light mode. Subtle outer shadow plus a strong inset top highlight that simulates a glass top edge.

shadows.name: glass-card-dark
shadows.purpose: Glass card in dark mode. Heavier outer shadow because there is no border, plus a faint inset highlight.

shadows.name: input-focus-light
shadows.purpose: 2px violet halo on focused inputs in light mode.

shadows.name: input-focus-dark
shadows.purpose: Layered violet halo with inset glow in dark mode — input feels illuminated rather than outlined.

shadows.name: field-dot-glow
shadows.purpose: Signal Teal glow under the field-group label dot when the field is focused. Same shadow with destructive color when invalid.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing — exponential ease-out is preferred for state transitions.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Field label color change, form aria-busy dim, dot glow fade.

motion.name: duration-feedback
motion.purpose: Password strength bar fill and shadow transitions.

breakpoints.name: coarse-pointer
breakpoints.purpose: Touch target floor of 2.75rem applied to button, [role='button'], and [data-slot='button'].

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Iris Violet primary button with scale(0.97) active press — the universal tactile feedback in the system.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.38 0.16 295); color: oklch(0.985 0 0); font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, transform 0.1s ease; min-height: 2.25rem; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.42 0.17 295); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.38 0.16 295); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Transparent at rest, accent surface on hover. Used in nav rows, command palette entries, account menu.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.145 0.006 285); font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, transform 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.945 0.014 285); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.38 0.16 295); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Field Group with Label Dot
components.description: The signature input pattern: a label with a 4px accent dot that turns Signal Teal on focus and destructive on invalid. Wrap every form input this way.
components.css: .ds-field-group { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-label { font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.145 0.006 285); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; transition: color 0.15s ease; } .ds-dot { display: inline-block; width: 4px; height: 4px; border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(0.908 0.007 285); margin-left: 6px; transition: background 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-input { background: oklch(0.975 0.004 265); color: oklch(0.145 0.006 285); border: 1px solid oklch(0.908 0.007 285); border-radius: 0.5rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; font-family: ui-monospace, 'Cascadia Code', 'SFMono-Regular', monospace; letter-spacing: 0.06em; font-size: 0.9375rem; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; outline: none; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: oklch(0.38 0.16 295 / 0.6); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.38 0.16 295 / 0.12); } .ds-field-group:focus-within .ds-label { color: oklch(0.55 0.12 195); } .ds-field-group:focus-within .ds-dot { background: oklch(0.55 0.12 195); box-shadow: 0 0 6px oklch(0.55 0.12 195); }

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: The signature card surface. CSS-mask trick paints a 1px violet→teal→neutral gradient border without an extra element. Card title gets a 3px violet→teal vertical pill before it.
components.html:
Sign in

Enter your username, master password, and authenticator code.
components.css: .ds-glass-card { position: relative; background: oklch(0.99 0.003 280); border: 1px solid oklch(0.908 0.007 285); border-radius: 0.625rem; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.06), 0 2px 4px -2px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.06), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.8); color: oklch(0.145 0.006 285); font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 28rem; } .ds-glass-card::before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; border-radius: inherit; padding: 1px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, oklch(0.38 0.16 295 / 0.3) 0%, oklch(0.55 0.12 195 / 0.15) 50%, oklch(0.6 0.008 265 / 0.2) 100%); -webkit-mask: linear-gradient(#fff 0 0) content-box, linear-gradient(#fff 0 0); -webkit-mask-composite: xor; mask-composite: exclude; pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.6; } .ds-card-header { padding-bottom: 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(0.908 0.007 285); } .ds-card-title { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Bricolage Grotesque Variable', 'Geist Variable', sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.25rem; line-height: 1.1; letter-spacing: -0.025em; margin: 0; } .ds-card-title::before { content: ''; display: block; width: 3px; height: 1em; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 9999px; background: linear-gradient(to bottom, oklch(0.38 0.16 295), oklch(0.55 0.12 195)); } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.9375rem; line-height: 1.5; color: oklch(0.556 0.008 285); margin: 0; }

components.name: Password Strength Bar
components.description: Four-segment strength readout. Segments fill teal at scores 1–2, violet at 3–4; score 4 adds a violet drop-shadow glow on the full bar.
components.css: .ds-pw-strength { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 4px; height: 3px; width: 12rem; } .ds-pw-strength > span { border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(0.908 0.007 285); transition: background 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-pw-strength[data-score='1'] > span:nth-child(-n+1), .ds-pw-strength[data-score='2'] > span:nth-child(-n+2) { background: oklch(0.55 0.12 195); } .ds-pw-strength[data-score='3'] > span:nth-child(-n+3) { background: oklch(0.38 0.16 295); } .ds-pw-strength[data-score='4'] > span { background: oklch(0.38 0.16 295); box-shadow: 0 0 6px oklch(0.38 0.16 295 / 0.4); }

components.name: Sidebar Panel
components.description: Glass-panel sidebar with violet-tinted background, hairline right border, and active route highlighted with Iris Violet text + accent-tinted surface.
components.html: VaultKeychainSyncSettings
components.css: .ds-sidebar { background: oklch(0.952 0.013 285); border-right: 1px solid oklch(0.902 0.008 285); padding: 0.5rem; min-width: 14rem; min-height: 16rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.125rem; backdrop-filter: blur(20px); font-family: 'Geist Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-item { display: block; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.145 0.006 285); padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: oklch(0.935 0.018 285); } .ds-nav-active { background: oklch(0.935 0.018 285); color: oklch(0.38 0.16 295); }

narrative.overview: BlindPass is a tool that holds the most sensitive material the user owns and refuses to perform. The interface is calm, slightly cool, and exact — slate-white in the light, blue-shifted obsidian in the dark, with a single violet accent (Iris Violet) that signals brand and a single teal (Signal Teal) that signals state. Cards are flat at rest. Type is structured, not loud. Motion is reserved for the few moments that genuinely matter: unlocking, locking, focus, sync resolution. The whole system is built so the math, not the marketing, is what reassures the user. Density is deliberate, with cozy and compact modes, and a 2.75rem touch-target floor on coarse pointers. A 3.5%-opacity fractal-noise overlay and a subtle radial violet glow live underneath every page; nothing about them ever lands in the foreground. The system explicitly rejects SaaS-cream consumer-PWM aesthetics, Bitwarden corporate-bootstrap, web3 neon-on-black, and — loudest forbidden — the untouched shadcn/v0 default look. shadcn is the substrate, not the design.
keyCharacteristics: OKLCH everywhere; no #000, no #fff, every neutral tinted toward violet (chroma 0.004–0.014).
Iris Violet is the brand. Signal Teal is the state accent. Two roles, never blurred.
Flat surfaces with tonal layering: background → list-panel → card → sidebar.
Glass effects are reserved for the three signature surfaces (.glass-card, .glass-panel, .glass-list-panel).
Mono-spaced password fields with 0.06em tracking — the type itself is the security cue.
Quiet by default, expressive at the moments that matter.

rules.name: The Two-Accent Rule
rules.body: The interface has exactly two saturated colors: Iris Violet and Signal Teal. Iris is identity; Signal is state. Do not introduce a third saturated color for decoration, status, severity beyond destructive, or 'data viz variety.'

rules.name: The OKLCH-Only Rule
rules.body: Every color in this codebase is OKLCH. #000, #fff, hex literals in component CSS, and HSL fallbacks are forbidden in product code. Rare exception: rgba(255,255,255, X) inset highlights inside .glass-card.

rules.name: The Tinted-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral has chroma between 0.003 and 0.018, biased toward violet/blue (hue 265–295). Pure greys (chroma 0) are forbidden in surface roles.

rules.name: The Mono-as-Cue Rule
rules.body: Mono is not for 'code-y' decoration. It appears only where the content itself is sensitive or literal: passwords, recovery phrases, key fingerprints, raw timestamps. The 0.06em letter-spacing makes glyph differences (l vs 1, O vs 0) unambiguous.

rules.name: The No-Gradient-Text Rule
rules.body: Headings are solid color, never background-clip: text over a gradient. Hierarchy is carried by family, weight, and tracking — not by chroma.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Most surfaces have zero shadow. Depth comes from tonal layering. Shadows ship only on .glass-card and as the focus glow on inputs/textarea/select.

rules.name: The Glass-Is-Earned Rule
rules.body: .glass-card, .glass-panel, and .glass-list-panel are the three sanctioned glass surfaces. Glassmorphism elsewhere is forbidden.

dos: Do use Iris Violet for identity and Signal Teal for state — never blur the two roles.
Do keep neutrals tinted (chroma 0.003–0.018, hue 265–295). Pure grey is forbidden.
Do use OKLCH in every new color declaration. Never #000, never #fff.
Do apply the field-group label dot to every new form input. State color reads through the dot.
Do switch password / recovery-phrase fields to mono with 0.06em tracking. Glyph clarity is a security feature.
Do keep the active-button scale(0.97) micro-press as the primary tactile feedback.
Do use tonal layering (background → list-panel → card → sidebar) for depth. Reach for a shadow only on .glass-card and input focus glows.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion. The global rule already neutralizes all animation/transition durations.
Do keep page text under 75ch line length. Settings prose, recovery instructions, and error explanations must wrap.
Do label state honestly. 'Vault locked.' 'Unsynced changes: 3.' 'Conflict on item github.com.' Cryptographic state is never softened.
donts: Don't ship the untouched shadcn/v0 default look. Slate is the wrong neutral; identical card grids are a smell. shadcn is the substrate, never the design.
Don't reach for SaaS-cream. No marketing pastels, no friendly illustrations, no onboarding confetti. BlindPass is not 1Password.
Don't introduce neon-on-black or web3 gradients. No animated hero blobs, no 'trustless' aesthetics. We prove security with the codebase, not with chrome.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored stripe on cards, list rows, or alerts.
Don't apply background-clip: text to a gradient on headings. Hierarchy is carried by Bricolage's weight and tracking, not by chroma.
Don't add glassmorphism beyond the three sanctioned surfaces.
Don't introduce a third saturated color. Extend Signal Teal's role or use destructive — never invent 'warning yellow' or 'info blue.'
Don't add celebratory toasts, success confetti, or progress animations on routine actions. Save motion for unlock, lock, sync resolution, and destructive confirmation.
Don't add em dashes or '--' in product copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't soften timestamps to 'a moment ago.' Show ISO time or exact relative durations.
Don't add 'Are you sure?' confirmations to non-destructive actions. Trust the operator.
Don't introduce hero-metric templates (big number, small label, supporting stats).
